subject: [PATCH v3 1/7] mm: list_lru: lock_list_lru_of_memcg() cannot return NULL if !skip_empty

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Acked the patch without any issues or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Mar 18, 2026 at 03:53:19PM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:
> skip_empty is only for the shrinker to abort and skip a list that's
> empty or whose cgroup is being deleted.
> 
> For list additions and deletions, the cgroup hierarchy is walked
> upwards until a valid list_lru head is found, or it will fall back to
> the node list. Acquiring the lock won't fail. Remove the NULL checks
> in those callers.
> 
> Reviewed-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>
> Signed-off-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes (Oracle)
reviews.summary: Approved the patch, noting that !skip_empty makes it impossible for lock_list_lru_of_memcg() to return NULL.

2026-03-18.patch_summary: The patch removes unnecessary NULL checks in callers of lock_list_lru_of_memcg() when !skip_empty, as the function cannot return NULL in those cases due to walking up the cgroup hierarchy or falling back to the node list.
